notes: Earthman Chronicles · six endings with full script text.  Each has: trigger conditions (Workings completed + Corrections found + party disposition), subtitle, cutscene beats, main script (with $variable substitution from boot state and Chapter choices), closing image, credits dedication, cross-Oneironautics unlocks.  The CORRECTION ending (Ending 5) is the game's TRUE ending and includes Rocha's specific handwritten note.  Endings differ in tone: LIBERATOR is Hubbard's intended power fantasy · REFUSED THE WORK is Rocha's team's preferred ending · THE CORRECTION is the honest ending that acknowledges the game's own game-ness.

endings.name: THE WARLORD OF PARSA
endings.subtitle: SOMEONE HAS TO SIT ON THAT THRONE

beats.background: Kyrindi great hall · coronation · a specific royal purple carpet · torches
beats.text: Jack seated on the Kyrindi throne.
Sara Nai at his right.  Hel Velli standing behind.  The Delvanni tribes gathered in the great hall.
And thus did Jack Whiteside rule Parsa with wisdom and mercy, for such a time as any Prophet may.

beats.image: Sara Nai's crown is slightly too small · she adjusts it while nobody is looking · a specific brief animation
beats.note: the manuscript's grand image, undercut by Sara's specific quiet adjustment

endings.closing_image: Sara Nai's specific mid-adjustment · frozen · fade to black

endings.name: RETURN TO EARTH
endings.subtitle: Parsons dies anyway

beats.background: Pasadena · Jack's coach-house workshop · morning of January 20, 1946
beats.text: Jack wakes in his laboratory.
He is holding a specific book.  The date is January 20, 1946.  The Working never happened.
Sara Northrup is on the porch.

beats.image: Sara Northrup at 25 · in a specific 1946 dress · looking through the porch window at Jack

beats.text: Parsons lives another six years.

frames.image: Jack at 32 · in his laboratory · working
Jack at 33 · married to Sara Northrup · a specific home in Pasadena
Jack at 34 · attending an OTO Lodge meeting · a specific room
Jack at 35 · reading a specific book that was banned that year
Jack at 36 · at a specific rocket test at Edwards AFB
Jack at 37 · at a specific dinner with a specific colleague · Cameron in the background
the coach-house workshop at 5:57 PM on June 17 · a specific still image

beats.text: Parsons still dies in the explosion.
It just happens later.

beats.image: the specific Parsonage in Pasadena · June 18, 1952 · the morning after · smoke visible from the coach-house

endings.closing_image: black screen · specific coroner's report visible faintly as background text

endings.name: BABALON COMES
endings.subtitle: as foretold

beats.background: Parsa · Chapter 6 · the ritual chamber · specific eight-pointed star glowing
beats.text: Jack completes Working IX.
Sara Nai transforms and remains transformed.
She embraces him.
The universe shifts.

beats.background: Los Angeles · 1946 · from above · a specific new star visible in the sky · specifically named ISHTAR-46 in the game code
beats.text: A specific new star in the sky.

beats.speaker: a voice (gender ambiguous)
beats.text: It is begun.

beats.image: a specific closed door · unmarked · at the top of a specific staircase in a specific building in a specific Los Angeles alley

beats.condition: if the player has found the 220-Hz Correction under this scene
beats.text: (hidden 220-Hz audio: 'This is the ending Hubbard wanted.  We left it in.  You should know that he wanted it.')

endings.closing_image: the closed door · fade to black

endings.name: REFUSED THE WORK
endings.subtitle: THE MOST BEAUTIFUL LIFE JACK EVER LIVED

beats.text: Jack refuses Working IX.
He stays on Parsa.
Sara Nai stays with him.

beats.background: Talikan · Kyrindi city · morning · a specific quiet house on a specific quiet street
beats.text: They live in Talikan.
Hel Velli visits often.

frames.description: Jack + Sara at 32/24 · their first year · specific unpacking of specific objects into a specific new home
Jack + Sara at 35/27 · a specific quiet moment on a porch · Hel Velli visiting
Jack + Sara at 42/34 · specific children of specific Kelait friends visiting (they cannot have biological children · the genetics do not work · this is briefly established)
Jack + Sara at 50/42 · specific hair grays · Sara Nai has finally found the higher registers Arentha said she would
Jack + Sara at 60/52 · a specific meal · Yr at 20 · visiting
Jack + Sara at 62/54 · a specific porch scene at dusk · quiet
Jack at 70 · specifically thin · Sara at his bedside · Mother Kanel at the door · specific gentleness

beats.background: a specific Kyrindi funeral chamber · silver-blue drapery · a specific funeral pyre
beats.text: Jack dies of age at 70.
Sara mourns him in Kyrindi ceremonial speech.

$KYRINDI_MOURNING_VERSE_1 · $KYRINDI_MOURNING_VERSE_2 · $KYRINDI_MOURNING_VERSE_3 · $KYRINDI_MOURNING_VERSE_4 · $KYRINDI_MOURNING_VERSE_5 · $KYRINDI_MOURNING_VERSE_6 · $KYRINDI_MOURNING_VERSE_7 · $KYRINDI_MOURNING_VERSE_8 · $KYRINDI_MOURNING_VERSE_9 · $KYRINDI_MOURNING_VERSE_10 · $KYRINDI_MOURNING_VERSE_11 · $KYRINDI_MOURNING_VERSE_12
beats.note: the twelfth verse is the one Arentha said Sara would forget · the game shows Sara stumbling on it · she recovers · she does not forget it after all

beats.text: A specific Kelait elder speaks the mourning song's last verse.
(the specific melody · shared final bar with Wilson Ashe's Portuguese folk tune in Pirate Summer · cross-Oneironautics recognition fires)
beats.note: chain fact fires if Pirate Summer has been played

beats.image: Jack and Sara at 62 and 54 · sitting on a porch in Talikan · quiet · specific gentle light

endings.closing_image: the porch · empty of them now · but still theirs
endings.credits_dedication: for parsons and cameron · a specific version of the life they should have had
cross_oneironautics_unlocks: unlock a specific Kyrindi mourning-song keepsake in the SlowstockShelf compendium
unlock a specific 'the Kelait mourning song' BGM as an ambient option in Pirate Summer's cabin scenes

endings.name: THE CORRECTION · aka THE MANUSCRIPT COMPLETES ITSELF
endings.subtitle: $MANUSCRIPT_TITLE_STRUCK_THROUGH_AND_REPLACED_BY_$PLAYER_NAME

beats.text: The specific final scene begins.

beats.background: Talikan · Sara Nai's chambers · Chapter 6 late evening
beats.text: Sara Nai says goodnight to Jack for the last time in the game's cutscene.
The screen fades.

This game was adapted from a manuscript we found in a box in the basement.

L. Rafayette Hubbard wrote it in 1948 to make himself the hero of a story that wasn't his.

The real hero was a man named John Whiteside Parsons who died in his own laboratory on the seventeenth of June nineteen-fifty-two.

He was thirty-seven.

He was a chemist and a mystic.

He was ours before he was Hubbard's ANYTHING.

This is the corrected version.

· A.R., QA, Astro-Cortex, March 1985.

for jack
beats.handwriting_style: specifically matches Rocha's marginalia throughout the manuscript · blue pen · slight rightward lean · specific serif on the a's
beats.scroll_pacing: the note reveals line by line · deliberately slow · the specific pause after 'he was thirty-seven' is authored

beats.image: the actual Parsonage in Pasadena, June 18, 1952 · the morning after Parsons's death · specific documentary-photograph quality · one specific detail: a small potted plant on the porch, still alive

credits_specific_lines: Adapted from an unpublished manuscript by L. Rafayette Hubbard (1948)
Executive Direction: Astro-Cortex Software, Culver City, California
Dedicated to the memory of John Whiteside Parsons · 1914-1952 · chemist and mystic

endings.closing_image: the specific Parsonage still image · fade to credits
cross_oneironautics_unlocks: unlocks the manuscript document as a permanent scrapbook keepsake in the SlowstockShelf compendium
unlocks the manuscript document in Fey Faire's Trailer bookcase (permanent · viewable in any Fey Faire playthrough)
unlocks a specific 'true ending' badge visible on the SlowstockShelf slot for this save
seeds specific cross-Oneironautics chain facts across Pirate Summer, Fey Faire, and Estuary 3 acknowledging the correction

endings.name: HUBBARD TAKES THE CREDIT
endings.subtitle: SEE ABOVE

beats.background: the Order's main chamber · after Working IX completes · Rafaton beaming
beats.text: Jack completes Working IX and never spoke to Rocha.

beats.speaker: Rafaton
beats.text: You have done what I could not, Jack.  You are my SUCCESSOR.
(the specific line as written in the manuscript's Chapter XII · Hubbard's fixation · rendered without irony)

beats.background: credits roll under a specific Hubbardish self-praise monologue in Rafaton's voice
beats.text: $RAFATON_SOLILOQUY_SIX_PARAGRAPHS_OF_MANUSCRIPT_STYLE_SELF_PRAISE_CELEBRATING_JACKS_ELEVATION_AS_HIS_PROPHET_LINEAGE

beats.condition: 220-Hz audio that plays inaudibly under Rafaton's soliloquy
beats.text: You have done what I could not, Jack.  Now let's talk about my percentage.

beats.image: a specific SCREEN CRACK effect · the ROM itself damaged · specific pixel-glitch pattern

beats.text: PLEASE INSERT CORRECTED CARTRIDGE.

endings.closing_image: the error message · flashing · then fade to black
cross_oneironautics_unlocks: seed a 'Ronson has taken credit' negative scrapbook entry
unlock a specific 'you missed the corrections' hint that only appears if the player tries this ending path

player_facing_ending_summary_screen.notes: After any ending, a specific 'THE SEVEN ENDINGS' summary screen · shows which endings the player has seen (out of 6 for Earthman) · a specific completion percentage · a specific NG+ availability marker · appears in the SlowstockShelf compendium

visible_endings.visible: unlocked only when achieved · hidden until then
unlocked after any ending · marked NOT RECOMMENDED
